seoTitle: Andrews vs Angas Plains — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Andrews vs Angas Plains: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Andrews and Angas Plains.
heading: Andrews vs Angas Plains — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Andrews, South Australia and Angas Plains, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Andrews has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Angas Plains (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Andrews or Angas Plains safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Andrews has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Angas Plains scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Andrews scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Angas Plains scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Andrews or Angas Plains?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Andrews has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
